name: cf-learn

criteria.name: knowledge_extraction
criteria.description: Captures the key insight or learning from the conversation/task

scoring.0: No meaningful knowledge extracted — note is trivial or obvious
scoring.1: Captures surface-level facts but misses the deeper insight or pattern
scoring.2: Captures the main insight but misses important nuances or context
scoring.3: Precisely extracts the non-obvious insight with proper context for future use

criteria.name: clarity
criteria.description: Learning is written clearly enough to be understood without original context

scoring.0: Note is incomprehensible without the original conversation
scoring.1: Note makes sense but uses jargon or references that need explanation
scoring.2: Note is clear but could be more self-contained
scoring.3: Note is fully self-contained, clear, and understandable by someone new to the project

criteria.name: structure
criteria.description: Note follows proper structure with title, category, and content

scoring.0: No structure — just a blob of text
scoring.1: Has a title but no category or proper formatting
scoring.2: Has title and category but content organization could be improved
scoring.3: Well-structured with clear title, correct category, and organized content

criteria.name: practical_value
criteria.description: Learning is actionable and will save time when encountered again

scoring.0: Learning has no practical value — just restates documentation
scoring.1: Learning is interesting but not actionable (no clear when/how to apply)
scoring.2: Learning is actionable but the triggering scenario is unclear
scoring.3: Learning clearly states when it applies, what to do, and why — saves real time in future

notes: Auto-invoked learnings should only fire on substantial new knowledge, not routine tasks. Category should match one of the configured categories. Learning should not duplicate existing notes.
